summary: Reviewed the event-fields UI audit against the supplied pack only. The audit correctly identifies that the five booking operations fields are absent from EventDetailSummary and gated behind canEdit, but several broader claims are not supported by the evidence in the pack. Overall, the main risk is false confidence: the audit treats form-name presence as end-to-end coverage and narrows UI coverage to detail/edit surfaces while naming broader UI/API surfaces in the concern.
appears_sound: The DB column count is internally consistent: the live schema lists 44 public.events columns, and the audit matrix accounts for 44 rows.

The stated read-only visibility gap for booking operations fields is supported by event detail page assembly: BookingSettingsCard is rendered only under canEdit while EventDetailSummary has no booking_enabled, total_capacity, max_tickets_per_booking, booking_url, or sms_promo_enabled rows.

The audit correctly avoids calling manager_responsible_id missing from EventDetailSummary a gap because the detail page header displays Manager responsible when event.manager_responsible_id is present.

findings.description: The audit's strongest claim, that 29 form fields are wired end-to-end from form to Zod to action to DB, is not proven by the pack. The pack includes only event-form field names and no src/actions/events.ts or validation schema contents, so save coverage could be materially overstated.
findings.evidence: The audit says “All wired end-to-end (form → Zod → action → DB),” but the pack only provides event-form.tsx field-name grep lines and not saveEventDraftAction or Zod schema code.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The omitted action and schema files may in fact handle every field exactly as claimed.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect src/actions/events.ts and src/lib/validation.ts to map every submitted field to validation and persisted DB columns.

findings.description: The audit treats all five booking settings as editable in the BookingSettingsCard, but sms_promo_enabled is administrator-only even for users who can edit the event. This means the role/data-state coverage is narrower than the audit summary implies.
findings.evidence: BookingSettingsCard renders the Promotional SMS toggle only when userRole === "administrator", and the action payload includes smsPromoEnabled only under the same condition.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The matrix note says “Toggle (admin only),” so this may be intended, but the summary count still overstates general edit coverage.
findings.what_would_confirm: Clarify whether “editable in booking settings card” means editable by any authorized user or only by administrators for sms_promo_enabled.

findings.description: The audit scope excludes list, board, calendar, public API, short-link, and email surfaces despite the user concern explicitly naming those surfaces. The pack shows the events list delegates rendering to EventsBoard, but EventsBoard contents are not included, so the claim that the only gap is on the detail page is not sufficiently tested.
findings.evidence: The audit scope is limited to event form, event detail page, and booking settings card, while the pack separately includes src/app/events/page.tsx returning <EventsBoard user={user} events={events} venues={venues} /> without that component’s fields.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The original audit may intentionally have a narrower scope and may not claim those other surfaces are complete.
findings.what_would_confirm: Review EventsBoard, any calendar components, public API serializers/routes, public landing pages, and notification templates against the same 44-column matrix.

findings.description: Several numeric fields are conditionally hidden when their value is 0, so the display audit overstates read visibility for valid zero states. In particular expected_headcount renders only when truthy, and a zero value would disappear rather than displaying 0 or an explicit unset state.
findings.evidence: EventDetailSummary uses {event.expected_headcount ? (...) : null}, while other numeric fields use != null checks for ticket_price, cancellation_window_hours, and cost_total.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The database or validation layer may disallow zero for expected_headcount, but that rule is not in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check validation and database constraints for expected_headcount and other numeric event fields to verify whether zero is a valid stored value.

findings.description: The audit asserts all 44 events table columns are accounted for, but the form grep includes artist subform fields that are not events columns. This does not by itself prove a missing event column, but it shows the evidence source mixes related-model fields with event fields and can hide mapping errors.
findings.evidence: The form field list includes name, artistType, email, phone, and description at event-form.tsx lines 1553-1615, none of which appear in the 44-column events schema.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The audit matrix itself does not count these artist fields as events columns, so the final table may still be correct.
findings.what_would_confirm: Trace only event-table fields through form defaults, submitted names, validation, and persistence, excluding related artist payloads.

empty_categories: No evidence in the pack that the 44-column schema list itself is incomplete.
No contradiction found for the audit’s treatment of system-managed fields as omitted from direct UI input.
No evidence in the pack of a security exposure from the proposed read-only display of booking operations fields.
